perturbation.original_text: The University of Texas M. D. Anderson Cancer Center, with a place of business located at 1515 Holcombe Blvd., Houston, TX 77030, USA ("MD Anderson"), a member institution of The University of Texas System ("System") and Adaptimmune LLC, with a place of business located at 2001 Market Street, Philadelphia, PA 1903, USA ("Adaptimmune"); and Adaptimmune Limited, with a place of business at 101 Milton Park, Abingdon, Oxfordshire, OX14 4RY ("Adaptimmune Limited") (MD Anderson and Adaptimmune each a "Party" and collectively the "Parties").
perturbation.changed_text: The University of Texas M. D. Anderson Cancer Center, with a place of business located at 1515 Holcombe Blvd., Houston, TX 77030, USA ("MD Anderson"), a member institution of The University of Texas System ("System") and Adaptimmune LLC, with a place of business located at 2001 Market Street, Philadelphia, PA 1903, USA ("Adaptimmune"); and Adaptimmune Limited, with a place of business at 101 Milton Park, Abingdon, Oxfordshire, OX14 4RY ("Adaptimmune Inc.") (MD Anderson and Adaptimmune each a "Party" and collectively the "Parties").
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Adaptimmune Limited" to "Adaptimmune Inc.", the contract introduces confusion as to whether both Adaptimmune LLC and "Adaptimmune Inc." are considered part of the collectively defined "Parties". This contradicts later sections that reference "Adaptimmune Limited", since this altered definition is not defined in this section and would now create confusion on if it reffers to 'Adaptimmune Inc' or not. The term 'Adaptimmune Limited' is no longer explicitly part of the Parties definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Inventions shall be owned by the Parties in accordance with the following: (a) *** "Adaptimmune Inventions" shall be the sole property of Adaptimmune Limited. (b) With respect to any Inventions that are not Adaptimmune Inventions ("Other Inventions"), where made solely by MD Anderson or its employees and agents, such Inventions will be solely owned by MD Anderson; where made jointly by MD Anderson and Adaptimmune and/or Adaptimmune Limited and their employees and agents will be jointly owned by MD Anderson and Adaptimmune Limited. Inventions that are made solely by Adaptimmune, Adaptimmune Limited or its employees and agents will be solely owned by Adaptimmune Limited. Inventorship will be determined in accordance with United States patent law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Adaptimmune Limited" as "Adaptimmune Inc.". This introduces an in-text contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly refers to "Adaptimmune Limited" regarding ownership of "Adaptimmune Inventions" and joint inventions. By changing the name, the contract creates uncertainty as to whether "Adaptimmune Inc." is the same entity as "Adaptimmune Limited" in the context of invention ownership, potentially invalidating or creating ambiguity around the ownership clauses. The explanation notes that the term 'Adaptimmune Limited' is no longer explicitly part of the Parties definition which makes it unclear as to who owns 'Adaptimmune Inventions'.

perturbation.original_text: Adaptimmune agrees to commit funding in an amount of at least nineteen million six hundred and forty four thousand Dollars US ($19,644,000) for the performance of the Studies as set out in Exhibit I during the term ("Alliance Funding"). The JSC may allocate and/or re-allocate funds to Studies as necessary and agreed by JSC.
perturbation.changed_text: Adaptimmune agrees to commit funding in an amount of at least nineteen million six hundred and forty four thousand Dollars US ($19,644,000) for the performance of the Studies as set out in Exhibit I during the term ("Research Grants"). The JSC may allocate and/or re-allocate funds to Studies as necessary and agreed by JSC.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Alliance Funding" with "Research Grants" creates uncertainty. The contract continues to use "Alliance Funding" in other sections, making it ambiguous whether "Research Grants" and "Alliance Funding" are interchangeable. This can affects downstream, if they are really the same entity. The fact that they are being reallocated by the JSC (Joint Steering Committee) creates an in-text contradiction where Alliance Funds is being reallocated in place of Research Grants, which creates confusion on how to reallocate the funds.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event a Study is terminated early, then in relation to any funds allocated to such Study, the Parties shall promptly discuss and agree upon a replacement of that Study with a new study of similar scope that is of mutual scientific interest to the Parties and that is approved by the JSC, and that will be funded by the Alliance Funding. If there is any Alliance Funding at the expiration or termination of this Agreement, it will be allocated to studies, research or tests agreed by the JSC, and such Alliance Funding will be payable in accordance with agreed milestones relevant to such agreed studies, research or tests. The Parties understand that the compensation being paid to MD Anderson under this Agreement constitutes the fair market value of the services to be provided hereunder. Neither MD Anderson nor Principal Investigator shall seek or accept reimbursement from any third-party payor for any Study items or procedures supplied by or paid for by Adaptimmune under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Alliance Funding' to 'Research Grants' introduces misaligned terminology. The 'Contradicted Text' refers exclusively to 'Alliance Funding'. If 'Research Grants' and 'Alliance Funding' are not explicitly defined as interchangeable, the Contradicted Text lacks clarity in regards to the funds that can be allocated in the event of early termination of a study. This creates a direct conflict and in-text contradiction as it is unclear whether funds allocated as 'Research Grants' can actually be reallocated according to the process defined for 'Alliance Funding' funds. The term misalignment creates ambiguity and uncertainty, therefore, it is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party can appoint and replace its representatives in the JSC at its own discretion through timely written notice to the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party can appoint and replace its delegates in the JSC at its own discretion through timely written notice to the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "representatives" to "delegates" introduces ambiguity. While the context suggests they might be the same, the contract doesn't explicitly define "delegates". This creates a potential conflict if a party argues that "delegates" have different rights or responsibilities than "representatives". The lack of consistency in terminology makes it unclear whether the rights, responsibilities, and voting power are the same between the two defined terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The JSC will have meetings (either in person, by teleconference or via electronic means) at least quarterly. At least one meeting per year will be conducted in person or by videoconference (including the kick-off meeting). The JSC will decide on matters by unanimous vote with each of MD Anderson and Adaptimmune exercising one vote each provided, however, that no action may lawfully be taken at any meeting unless at least two representatives of each Party (including for this purpose any proxy representative appointed as provided below) are present at the meeting. If a member of the JSC is unable to attend a meeting, he or she may appoint, in writing, a proxy to participate and vote in his or her stead. Decisions may also be made by electronic mail, provided such electronic mail is provided by at least two representatives from Adaptimmune and MD Anderson and such electronic mail is acknowledged to be received by the recipient. Although decision will be made by mutual agreement of the JSC, *** .
perturbation.justification: The change from 'representatives' to 'delegates' introduces misaligned terminology, as the contract doesn't define 'delegates'. The contradicted text specifically refers to 'representatives' concerning meeting attendance and voting power. If 'delegates' are not explicitly defined as having the same rights and responsibilities as 'representatives', a party could argue that 'delegates' cannot fulfill the 'representatives' requirements for quorum or proxy appointments as stated in the contradicted text, creating a direct contradiction regarding meeting procedures and the validity of decisions made by the JSC.
